channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Steep rise in civilian casualties in Russia-Ukraine war, UN says
item.description: Increasing use of long-range missiles and drones against targets far from the front line is worsening the civilian toll.

item.title: US appeals court overturns ruling that freed Columbia activist Mahdawi
item.description: The decision is the latest court victory for US President Donald Trump’s campaign targeting foreign student protesters.

item.title: US eyes new tariffs as existing trade duties near expiration
item.description: US Trade Rep Jamieson Greer hints at new tariffs on 60 trading partners as Trump's temporary tariffs expire on Friday.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni slams protesters following clashes in Bologna
item.description: PM pledges probe into death of Moroccan man under police restraint, condemns violence as protests injure 56 officers.

item.title: UK PM Burnham: ‘We need to be a cost-of-living government’
item.description: At his first cabinet meeting as UK prime minister, Burnham told ministers they must be a "cost-of-living government".

item.title: FIFA ‘destroying football’ for the World Cup, says Spanish football chief
item.description: In a scathing interview, La Liga President Javier Tebas says FIFA chief Gianni Infantino’s time is 'over'.

item.title: Israel has ‘no interest’ in joining US war on Iran, says Smotrich
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister says Israel has "no interest" in joining the renewed US military campaign against Iran.

item.title: Saudi condemns Houthi blockade: How will the rest of the world be impacted?
item.description: Saudi Arabia has condemned the Houthi naval blockade that could impact oil flow to importers of Saudi oil.

item.title: Searching for closure: Venezuela families refuse to give up after quakes
item.description: Weeks after earthquakes, relatives are still searching for bodies of loved ones who disappeared beneath the rubble.

item.title: Japan’s AI gamble: Can technology offset the cost of an ageing society?
item.description: From underground networks to emerging AI start-ups, Japan is testing whether its greatest issue can be an opportunity.

item.title: ASEAN diplomats voice ‘serious concern’ over Iran war and energy crisis
item.description: Southeast Asian countries are highly affected by oil supply shortages caused by Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: What does a heatwave cost?
item.description: What does a heatwave cost?

item.title: Clashes between police and people protesting Italy arrest death
item.description: Clashes between police and people protesting Italy arrest death

item.title: India tunnel collapse at a hydropower project kills 10 as 17 remain missing
item.description: Rescue teams have been struggling to reach the 17 people trapped because of hazardous conditions inside the tunnel.

item.title: ‘There is ‌no safe place’: Israeli attack kills family of six in Gaza
item.description: Israeli forces launch regular attacks on Gaza despite a US-backed 'ceasefire' agreed in October.

item.title: Trump’s new 50 percent Canada tariffs: What products are affected and why?
item.description: US leader imposes new sweeping tariffs on Canada, straining already tense ties with its second-largest trading partner.

item.title: Bodycam shows Florida student tased and arrested by campus police
item.description: Bodycam shows Florida student tased and arrested by campus police

item.title: Baby trafficking ringleader jailed for 7 years
item.description: An Indonesian court has sentenced Lie Siu Luan to seven years in prison for leading a baby trafficking ring.

item.title: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in US prison
item.description: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in US prison

item.title: Iran war: Look beyond stocks to understand state of economy, experts say
item.description: Yields on 10-year US Treasury bills, a bellwether for inflation, are up 60 basis points since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: How a 64-team World Cup could transform football’s economics
item.description: Spain, Portugal and Morocco are the cohosts of the 2030 edition, but we await confirmation of 48 or 64 competitors.

item.title: India’s ‘cockroach’ protest movement halts marches after police violence
item.description: Leaders of the movement say they will continue campaigning but will not stage another march through New Delhi.

item.title: Hunger is falling but is still higher than pre-COVID levels: UN report
item.description: Global hunger has declined, yet millions of people are still going hungry.

item.title: Hamas’s new leader must bridge a movement divided over Iran
item.description: After a one-vote victory, Khalil al-Hayya must reconcile rival currents while preserving Hamas’s independence.

item.title: ‘Won’t back down’: Indian student protesters return after police crackdown
item.description: More than 100 students injured in police action against thousands of demonstrators during march on parliament.
